description_long: This is a large wooden half-pipe skate ramp, commonly found in urban parks or recreational areas. The structure features two high vertical ramps on either end, connected by a wide, gently curved central section, and is constructed primarily from wood panels with visible seams and patches. Metal guardrails run along the top edges for safety, and a staircase with yellow steps leads up to a platform on one side, providing access to the ramp. The surfaces are adorned with graffiti art and tags, adding an urban, well-used character. There is a central cutout or opening in the platform, possibly for maintenance or as an additional play feature. The ramp shows signs of wear, such as scuff marks and discoloration, indicating frequent use.
description: A large wooden half-pipe skate ramp with metal guardrails, graffiti, and a staircase leading to a raised platform. The structure is urban, well-worn, and designed for skateboarding or similar activities.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the half-pipe, graffiti on the side panels, and the staircase with yellow steps leading to the top platform.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the curved ramp surface, central opening, and the staircase on the right side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying more graffiti, the top platform, and the 'No Entry' sign on the stair tower.
description_view_3: View from the other end, focusing on the ramp's interior, graffiti on the ramp surface, and the staircase structure.
